title: Bacterial DNA extraction using modified Zymobiomics for short- and long-read sequencing
author: Thidathip Wongsurawat

steps.title: Add 750 µl ZymoBIOMICS‱ Lysis Solution...
steps.instruction: Add 750 µl ZymoBIOMICS‱ Lysis Solution to the ZR BashingBead‱ Lysis Tubes. 1.2 Pick up bacterial isolates (whole plate) using inoculate loop and add them to the tube and cap tightly. 1.

steps.title: Secure in a bead beater or...
steps.instruction: Secure in a bead beater or vortex with a 2 ml tube and process at maximum speed for 3 minutes (modified from original protocol which suggests to beat for 20 minutes). 1.

steps.title: Centrifuge the ZR BashingBead‱ Lysis Tubes...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the ZR BashingBead‱ Lysis Tubes in a microcentrifuge at ≥ 10,000 x g for 1 minute. 1.

steps.title: Transfer up to 400 µl supernatant...
steps.instruction: Transfer up to 400 µl supernatant to the Zymo-Spin‱ III-F Filter in a Collection Tube and centrifuge at 8,000 x g for 1 minute. Discard the Zymo-Spin‱ III-F Filter. Discard the Zymo-Spin‱ III-F Filter. 1.

steps.title: Add 1,200 μl of ZymoBIOMICS‱ DNA...
steps.instruction: Add 1,200 μl of ZymoBIOMICS‱ DNA Binding Buffer to the filtrate in the Collection Tube (from Step 1.5 Step 1.5). Mix well. 1.

steps.title: Transfer 800 µl of the mixture...
steps.instruction: Transfer 800 µl of the mixture from Step 5 to a Zymo-Spin‱ IIC-Z Column in a Collection Tube and centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute. 1.

steps.title: Discard the flow through from the...
steps.instruction: Discard the flow through from the Collection Tube and repeat Step 1.7 repeat Step 1.

steps.title: Add 400 µl ZymoBIOMICS‱ DNA Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µl ZymoBIOMICS‱ DNA Wash Buffer 1 to the Zymo-Spin‱ IIC-Z Column in a new collection Tube and centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute. Discard the flow-through. 1.

steps.title: Add 700 µl ZymoBIOMICS‱ DNA Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 700 µl ZymoBIOMICS‱ DNA Wash Buffer 2 to the Zymo-Spin‱ IIC-Z Column in a Collection Tube and centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute. Discard the flow-through. 1.

steps.title: Add 200 µl ZymoBIOMICS‱ DNA Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl ZymoBIOMICS‱ DNA Wash Buffer 2 to the Zymo-Spin‱ IIC-Z Column in a Collection Tube and centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute. 1.

steps.title: Transfer the Zymo-Spin‱ IIC-Z Column to a clean 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the Zymo-Spin‱ IIC-Z Column to a clean 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube and add 100 µl (50 µl minimum) NFW directly to the column matrix and incubate for 1 minute. Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 1 minute to elute the DNA. 1.

steps.title: Place a Zymo-Spin‱ III-HRC Filter in...
steps.instruction: Place a Zymo-Spin‱ III-HRC Filter in a new Collection Tube and add 600 µl ZymoBIOMICS‱ HRC Prep Solution. Centrifuge at 8,000 x g for 3 minutes. 1.

steps.title: Transfer the eluted DNA (Step 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the eluted DNA (Step 1.12 Step 1.

steps.title: to a prepared Zymo-Spin‱ III-HRC Filter in a clean 1
steps.instruction: to a prepared Zymo-Spin‱ III-HRC Filter in a clean 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube and centrifuge at 16,000 x g for 3 minutes. The filtered DNA is now ready for the cleanup step.

steps.title: Allow magnetic bead reagent to come...
steps.instruction: Allow magnetic bead reagent to come to room temperature prior to use. 2.

steps.title: Mix the reagent well before use
steps.instruction: Mix the reagent well before use. It should appear homogenous and consistent in color (called mixture). 2.

steps.title: Add 1 volume of the mixture...
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume of the mixture to 1 volume of sample. For example, adding 80 µl bead to 80 µl extracted DNA. 2.

steps.title: Gently pipette up and down 10...
steps.instruction: Gently pipette up and down 10 times and gently flick the tube for 5 times then spin down (1-2 second). 2.

steps.title: Incubate the mixture at room temperature for 5-10 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate the mixture at room temperature for 5-10 minutes. 2.

steps.title: Place the mixture onto magnetic separation...
steps.instruction: Place the mixture onto magnetic separation rack for 3 minutes to separate beads from solution 2.

steps.title: Prepare fresh 80% ethanol (400 µl...
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh 80% ethanol (400 µl ethanol and 100 µl NFW). 2.

steps.title: Aspirate the cleared solution from the reaction tube and discard
steps.instruction: Aspirate the cleared solution from the reaction tube and discard. This step must be performed while the reaction tube is situated on the magnetic separation rack. Do not disturb the area of magnetic beads. 2.

steps.title: Dispense 200 µl of 80% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Dispense 200 µl of 80% ethanol to reaction tube and incubate for 1 minute at room temperature. Aspirate out the ethanol and discard. Repeat for a total of two washes. 2.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic separation rack and spin down the tube (1 second).Then, place the tube onto magnetic separation rack and use 10 µl pipette to aspirate out the remained ethanol and discard. 2.

steps.title: Place the reaction tube on bench...
steps.instruction: Place the reaction tube on bench top to air-dry for 3-10 minutes. Be sure to allow the dry (not shiny). 2.

steps.title: Add 40 µl of nuclease free...
steps.instruction: Add 40 µl of nuclease free water to each tube of the reaction and flicking 5 times or until no clump and perform spin down (1-2 second). Incubate for 2 minutes. 2.

steps.title: Place the tube onto magnetic separation...
steps.instruction: Place the tube onto magnetic separation rack and use 10 µl pipette to aspirate out the DNA in the NFW and put into the new tube. 2.

steps.title: Label and aliquot purified DNA into...
steps.instruction: Label and aliquot purified DNA into 2 tubes (for Illumina and Nanopore). Store aliquoted DNA at -20°C, unless proceeding to the downstream application.

steps.title: Purity
steps.instruction: Purity: Apply 1 µl of DNA. Use the NanoDrop‱ to measure absorbance and record sample purity. The OD 260/280 ratio should be 1.8 and OD 260/230 should be 2.0-2.

steps.title: Concentration
steps.instruction: Concentration: Apply 1 µl of DNA. Use the Qubit‱ to measure concentration (reference no. 3). 3.

steps.title: Integrity
steps.instruction: Integrity: Apply 1-5 µl of DNA. Use the 0.8% Agarose gel to check the integrity (reference no. 4). Load 1 kb DNA ladder on the first lane and high molecular weight DNA ladder on the last lane. After running the gel electrophoresis, high integrity of DNA will locate above 10 kb in the area of high molecular weight DNA ladder.
